Withdrawal of candidature.-( 1) Any candidate may withdraw his candidature by notice in writing in Form VII-A signed by him and delivered in person by him or hy his election agent to the Returning Officer before four O'clock in the afternoon on the day following the scrutiny of nomination papers under sub-rule (1) of rule 26: Pmv i ded that if scrutiny is undertaken by nominee ol Returning Officer and the person aggrieved prefers an appeal before the President, in such cases the date of with- drawal shall be the day, following the day the President decides the appeal. (4) A candidate who has given notice of withdrawal of his candidature under sub-rule (1) shall not be allowed to cancel the notice, or to be renominated as a candidate for the same election. Procedure in contested and un-contested elec- tions.-After the hour for withdrawal provided in sub-rule (1) of rule 27 has expired, if- (a) The number of contesting candidates in a ward is more than one, a poll shall be taken; (b) The number of such candidates is one, the Returning Officer shall forthwith declare such candidate to be duly elected. 29. Challenging ofidentity.--(1) Any candidate or his election agent may challenge the identity of a person cla1rning to be a particular elector by first depositing a sum of :me hundred rupees in cash with the Polling Officer for each such challenge. 31 (2) On such deposit being made, the Pollmg Officer shall- (a) warn the person challenged, the penalty for im- personation : (b) read the relevant entry in the electoral roll in full and ask him whether he is the person referred to in that entry : (c) enter his name and address in the list of chal- lenged votes in Form X : and (d) require him to affix his signature in the said list. (3) The Polling Officer shall thereafter hold a sum- mary inquiry into the challenge and may for that purpose- ( a) require the challenger to adduce evidence in proof of the challenge and the person challenged to adduce evidence in proof of his identity'; (b) put to the person challenged any questions nec- essary for the purpose of establishing his iden- tity and require him to answer them on oath; and (c) administer an oath to the person challenged and any other person offering to give evidence.
